name: Agnolotti al Limone
description: Silky agnolotti served in a lemon cream sauce.  The Limoncello makes it a little sweet to balance the savory ricotta
keywords: pasta, ravioli, lemon, dinner, italian

winePairings.name: Verdicchio
winePairings.notes: Stony minerality balances lemon cream richness.

winePairings.name: Antinori Cervaro della Sala
winePairings.notes: Luxurious but bright enough for citrus.

steps.instruction: In a large pot, bring 4 quarts water and 1 cup kosher salt to a boil over high heat.
steps.note: The water should smell like the ocean

steps.instruction: Mix the ricotta, 1 egg, salt, white pepper, Garlic Powder, and lemon juce with a fork.
Spoon the ricotta mixture about 1/2 inch from the edge of the pasta sheet, about 2 inches apart.
Fold the pasta sheet to cover the mixture and pinch the long edge.
Press between each ricotta spoonful to make individual agnolotti
Cut with a ravioli cutter
steps.note: a knife could also be used if a straight edge is preferred.

steps.instruction: Repeat for all pasta sheets
Place in the boiling water for about 1 minute.
steps.note: the agnolotti will float when done

steps.instruction: Ladle Limoncello Cream Sauce into each bowl.
Garnish with Parsley.
Melt butter in a sauce pan over medium heat.
Add minced garlic to soften
steps.note: Do not allow garlic to brown

steps.instruction: Whisk in lemon juice
Slowly whisk in heavy cream
Once combined remove from heat
Add Limoncello and return to stove over low heat
Cook until thickened
steps.note: Do not boil

name: Agnolotti del Plin
description: An iconic dish from Piemonte, the pasta is filled with veal, pork, spinach, Parmigiano Reggiano, and eggs, then served in a sage butter sauce.
keywords: pasta, italian, piemontese, agnolotti, filled pasta

winePairings.name: Barbera d’Alba
winePairings.notes: Bright acid cuts butter, cherry notes complement veal.

winePairings.name: Gaja Barbaresco
winePairings.notes: Nebbiolo tannins + truffle/sage aromatics = perfect Piedmont pairing.

steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the agnolotti for 3–4 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Meanwhile, melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add sage leaves and toast about 1 minute, then remove the leaves.
Add 1 cup of pasta water to the butter, simmer for 2 minutes until reduced by half. Keep warm over low heat.

Drain the agnolotti and toss them in the butter sauce. Cook 1 minute more over medium heat until the sauce bubbles. Remove from heat, add grated cheese, and serve immediately with shaved truffle if using.
Sift and mound the flour on a work surface. Make a well in the center and add the eggs.
Using a fork, beat the eggs and gradually incorporate the flour from the inner rim of the well.
Knead the dough with your palms for about 15 minutes until cohesive and elastic, adding flour as needed.
Wrap in plastic wrap and rest for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Bring 6 quarts of water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
In a saucepan, melt butter over high heat. Add garlic and rosemary; cook until garlic is light golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Add veal and pork; brown on all sides, stirring occasionally for 8–10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
Cook spinach in boiling water for 1 minute, drain well, and add to meat.
Stir in Parmigiano Reggiano, eggs, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Cut the dough into 3 pieces. Roll each piece into a thin sheet about 1/8 inch thick.
Place spoonfuls of filling along the bottom half of the pasta sheet, spaced 1½ inches apart.
Fold the top edge of pasta over the filling to form one large pocket. Press gently to seal, removing air pockets.
Seal the sides and use your fingers to pinch between mounds of filling at 1-inch intervals to form individual agnolotti.
Cut along the bottom edge of dough and separate individual agnolotti with a pastry wheel. Place on a cornmeal-dusted tray.
Repeat with remaining dough and filling. Let rest for 20–30 minutes before cooking.

name: Asparagus Pavé with Brown Butter Sabayon, Morels & Caviar
description: A structural culinary masterpiece featuring precisely layered and compressed asparagus spears, slow-cooked sous-vide to velvet tenderness with a glass-like crisp exterior. Finished with a light, airy brown butter sabayon, sautéed morel mushrooms, and crowned with luxurious caviar.
keywords: asparagus pavé, michelin style, brown butter sabayon, morels, caviar, molecular gastronomy, haute cuisine

ingredients.note: uniform white or green spears, preferably high-end producer

steps.instruction: Snap off woody ends of asparagus. Using a Japanese Vegetable Turning Slicer or dedicated asparagus peeler, create uniform ribbons or thin continuous sheets. Blanch ribbons and whole peeled spears for 45-60 seconds in salted boiling water, then shock in ice bath and pat completely dry.

Brush each asparagus ribbon with clarified butter, season with sea salt, white pepper, and finely chopped tarragon. Layer tightly in a rectangular terrine mold or ring molds lined with parchment, alternating direction to create 40-50 precise layers.
Cover with buttered parchment and vacuum-seal the mold in a chamber vacuum sealer on high pressure to compress and bond the layers.
Cook the sealed pavé in a precision immersion circulator at 82°C (179.6°F) for 25-30 minutes. Shock in ice bath, then refrigerate under a 1-2kg weight for at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.

Prepare brown butter sabayon: Brown 100g butter until nutty. Whisk egg yolks with white wine, lemon juice, and salt over a bain-marie until tripled in volume. Slowly incorporate brown butter. Pass through a fine chinois. Transfer to the Gourmet Whip, charge with 1-2 N2O canisters, and hold warm at 60°C.
Sauté morels in clarified butter with minced shallot over high heat until golden. Season lightly and keep warm.
Unmold the chilled pavé and cut into clean 2-inch rectangles. Sear two sides in a hot carbon steel or copper pan with clarified butter until deep golden and glass-like crisp.

Plating: Place the seared asparagus pavé off-center on a pristine white plate. Dispense a delicate cloud of warm brown butter sabayon beside it. Arrange sautéed morels artistically, crown with caviar, Meyer lemon zest, micro tarragon/chervil, and Maldon flakes. Finish with optional white truffle oil.

specialtools.item: High-end Copper or Carbon Steel Pan

winePairings.name: Chablis Grand Cru
winePairings.notes: Mineral-driven Chardonnay that echoes the fresh asparagus and rich sabayon.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: High acidity perfectly balances the caviar, brown butter, and luxurious textures.

name: Avocado Royale
description: An elegant avocado-centric brunch course featuring vacuum-compressed Hass avocado carpaccio, sweet Dungeness crab, a custardy 63°C sous-vide quail egg, and a light truffle-infused avocado espuma.
keywords: avocado, crab, quail egg, brunch, fine dining

winePairings.name: Chablis or crisp Chardonnay
winePairings.notes: Mineral acidity cuts through the rich avocado and crab while highlighting the truffle notes.

winePairings.name: Rosé Champagne
winePairings.notes: Bubbly elegance complements the luxurious textures and caviar pops.

ingredients.note: or fresh yuzu zest + pinch of chili

ingredients.item: Fine sea salt and white pepper

steps.instruction: Prepare the compressed avocado carpaccio and avocado-truffle espuma
steps.note: see sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Sous-vide the quail eggs
steps.note: see sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Gently warm the Dungeness crab meat with a touch of yuzu juice and salt. Do not overcook.
Peel the sous-vide quail eggs carefully just before plating.
Season components lightly with salt and white pepper as needed.
Plating Layer 1: Place the compressed avocado carpaccio disc in the center of the plate using a 3-inch ring mold.
Plating Layer 2: Add a thin even layer of warm crab meat on the avocado base.
Plating Layer 3: Carefully place one peeled sous-vide quail egg on top of the crab. Remove the ring mold.
Dispense generous clouds of avocado-truffle espuma over and around the egg with the iSi siphon.
Spoon Ossetra caviar in an elegant line across the espuma. Shave remaining fresh black truffle generously over the plate.
Finish with tiny dots of yuzu gel, micro shiso leaves, and delicate flakes of edible gold leaf applied with micro tweezers.
Peel and thinly slice each avocado into ?-inch rounds using a sharp knife or mandoline.
Arrange overlapping slices inside a 3-inch ring mold on the base of a vacuum bag. Top with another layer of slices to enclose.
Place in the chamber vacuum sealer and apply full vacuum + 2-second seal. Compress for 30 seconds.
Gently remove the ring mold. The avocado will hold a perfect glossy disc. Chill for 15 minutes.
In a double boiler, whisk egg yolks with yuzu juice until pale and thickened.
Slowly emulsify in the warm oil to form a light base. Blend in reserved avocado flesh and yuzu kosho until silky.
Season with salt and white pepper. Grate 10 g fresh black truffle directly into the mixture.
Transfer to iSi siphon, charge with two N?O cartridges, shake vigorously 12–15 times. Keep warm in 50°C water bath.
Set sous-vide immersion circulator to exactly 63°C / 145°F.
Gently lower quail eggs (in shell) into the stabilized water bath.
Cook for 18 minutes for a tender white and custardy, runny yolk.
Shock briefly in ice water. Peel carefully just before plating.

name: Baked camembert with roasted grapes, walnuts, rosemary
description: Gooey, melted camembert is pierced with aromatic rosemary sprigs and topped with toasted walnuts and roasted grapes
keywords: cheese, grapes, appetizer, dessert

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C)
Place flour, half the pink peppercorns, and salt into a bowl.
Add the oil and water and stir together until a dough forms
Knead the dough until smooth.  Cover and rest for 10 minutes
Place the grapes on a tray and drizzle with vegetable oil.
Roast for 10 minutes or until skin splits and grapes are oozing.
Open the Camembert box and remove the paper wrapping from the cheese.
Place Camembert back into the box and place on an ovenproof tray
Cut diagonal slashes into the top of the cheese.
Place the rosemary into the slashes and spring with walnuts.
Drizzle with some vegetable oil.
Roll out the dough so it is 2-3 mm (about 1 inch) thick
Score the dough into triangle shapes.
steps.note: the triangles do not need to be separated - it will automatically happen in the oven

steps.instruction: Transfer to a flat baking tray and brush with olive oil
Sprinkle with sea salt and remaining pink peppercorns
Bake for 5-8 minutes
Once the grapes and crackers are done, put the Camembert into the oven
Bake for 10-15 minutes
Add the roasted graps on top and serve with crackers

name: Baked Littleneck Clams with Brown Butter, Garlic Confit & Herb Pangrattato
description: A Michelin-level baked clams dish featuring tender littlenecks, silky brown butter emulsion, sweet garlic confit, and a crisp herb pangrattato topping.
keywords: baked clams, fine dining, seafood, Michelin, garlic confit, pangrattato

steps.instruction: Prepare garlic confit by slowly cooking garlic, olive oil, thyme, and lemon zest at low heat until soft and aromatic.
Steam clams with white wine until just opened, then remove top shells and strain reserved liquid.
Make pangrattato by saut�ing shallots and anchovy in clarified butter, then adding breadcrumbs and toasting until golden. Finish with herbs and lemon zest.
Prepare brown butter by cooking butter until nutty and golden, then blending with garlic confit, lemon juice, white wine, and a touch of clam liquor.
Preheat oven to 450�F and spread kosher salt on a tray to hold clams steady.
Place clams on tray, spoon brown butter emulsion over each, and top with pangrattato.
Bake for 5�7 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Finish with Maldon salt, Espelette pepper, and fresh herbs before serving.

winePairings.name: Chablis (Burgundy)
winePairings.notes: Crisp minerality and citrus cut through the richness of the butter.

winePairings.name: Vermentino
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and herbal notes complement the clams and herbs.

steps.instruction: Combine all ingredients and cook gently at low heat until garlic is soft and lightly golden.
Cook shallots and anchovy in butter, add breadcrumbs, toast until golden, then finish with herbs.

name: Ballotine of Chicken with Black Truffle and Jus Gras
description: A luxurious Bresse chicken ballotine infused with black truffle, served with a rich jus gras made from roasted chicken bones, vegetables, and truffle. Elegant, earthy, and perfect for special occasions.
keywords: bresse chicken, chicken, ballotine, truffle, jus gras, gourmet, french

winePairings.name: White Burgundy (Mâcon-Villages)
winePairings.notes: Enough body for the jus; bright mineral notes for truffle.

winePairings.name: Montrachet Grand Cru
winePairings.notes: Benchmark pairing with truffle + luxurious poultry.

steps.instruction: Slice the cooked ballotine into thick medallions.
Spoon a generous amount of jus gras on the plate.
Arrange chicken slices on top. Garnish with micro herbs, truffle slices, or edible flowers if desired. Serve immediately.

ingredients.note: about 3–4 lbs, skin on.  If you cannot find a Bresse chicken, use a high quality whole chicken.  Boneless skin on chicken breasts may also be used if deboning a chicken is difficult

steps.instruction: Debone the chicken, keeping the skin intact to form a flat piece of meat.
steps.note: if using a chicken breast, butterfly andd flatten with a meat tenderizer.

steps.instruction: Prepare truffle butter: finely mince 50 g of truffle and mix with softened butter, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Spread truffle butter evenly over the flattened chicken. Place remaining truffle slices on top.
Roll chicken tightly and secure with kitchen twine or sausage netting.
Cook: either poach in a water bath at 160°F (70°C) for 30–40 min, or roast in oven at 350°C (180°C) for 35–45 min until internal temp reaches 150°F.
Remove from heat and rest 10 minutes before slicing carefully.

sub_recipes.name: Jus Gras (Rich Chicken and Truffle Sauce)

steps.instruction: Roast chicken bones, wings, and vegetables at 400°F (205°C) until browned (20–25 min).
steps.note: if using chicken breasts, skip this step.

steps.instruction: Deglaze pan with white wine and reduce by half.
Add chicken stock, simmer 20–30 min. Strain and reduce to concentrate flavor.
Off heat, whisk in cold butter cubes to emulsify.
Stir in finely diced black truffle and season to taste.

name: Beef Tenderloin with Bone Marrow Sabayon
description: A luxurious main course pairing perfectly seared beef tenderloin with a silky, umami-rich bone marrow sabayon and red wine reduction.
keywords: beef, tenderloin, bone marrow, sabayon, fine dining, french, gourmet

winePairings.name: Washington State Merlot
winePairings.notes: Plush dark fruit supports marrow richness.

winePairings.name: Penfolds Grange
winePairings.notes: Massive structure + savory elements complement marrow.

steps.instruction: Spoon red wine reduction onto warm plates.
Place sliced tenderloin atop the sauce.
Top with a generous spoonful of bone marrow sabayon.
Garnish with micro herbs and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Season steaks generously with salt and pepper.
Sear in a cast iron skillet coated in olive oil over high heat until well-browned on all sides.
Add butter, thyme, and garlic; baste for 1–2 minutes.
Transfer to oven and cook until internal temperature reaches 54°C (129°F).
Rest 5–10 minutes before slicing.
Roast marrow bones at 200°C (400°F) for 15–20 minutes until soft and releasing fat.
Scoop out marrow and strain through fine mesh to remove impurities.
In a heatproof bowl over simmering water, whisk egg yolks and wine until thick and foamy.
Slowly whisk in warm melted bone marrow fat to create an emulsion.
Season with lemon juice, salt, and white pepper.
Keep warm over a bain-marie until ready to serve.
Sauté shallot in a small pan until translucent.
Add wine and thyme; reduce by two-thirds.
Add beef stock and reduce until thick and glossy.
Strain and whisk in cold butter just before serving.

name: Beef Wellington
description: A world-class chef execution of Beef Wellington featuring perfectly cooked beef tenderloin, mushroom duxelles, prosciutto, and golden puff pastry with precision layering and moisture control.
keywords: beef wellington, fine dining, gourmet, steak, pastry

winePairings.name: Bordeaux (Left Bank)
winePairings.notes: Cabernet Sauvignon structure complements the richness of the beef and pastry.

winePairings.name: Barolo
winePairings.notes: High tannin and acidity balance the fat and umami of the dish.

specialtools.item: Perforated baking tray or silicone mat

steps.instruction: Season the beef tenderloin with salt and pepper and sear in a hot pan with oil until deeply browned on all sides.
Brush the seared beef with Dijon mustard while warm and allow to cool.
Optional: Vacuum seal and cook sous vide at 125°F (52°C) for 45–60 minutes, then chill completely.
Prepare the duxelles by finely chopping mushrooms in a food processor.
Cook mushrooms with butter, shallots, garlic, and thyme until all moisture evaporates. Deglaze with sherry and cook dry.
Spread duxelles on a tray and chill.
Make crepes by whisking eggs, flour, milk, and salt, then cook thin layers in a buttered pan.
Lay out plastic wrap, overlap prosciutto slices, spread duxelles evenly, then place crepes on top.
Place beef at the edge and roll tightly into a log using plastic wrap. Chill for 20 minutes.
Roll puff pastry to even thickness and wrap around the beef, sealing edges tightly.
Brush with egg wash and score the pastry lightly for decoration.
Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 20–25 minutes until golden brown.
Cook until internal temperature reaches 120–125°F (49–52°C) for medium rare.
Rest for 10–15 minutes before slicing.
Slice carefully with a serrated knife and plate with sauce and sides.
Reduce wine and shallots by half over medium heat.
Add veal stock and reduce until thick and glossy.
Finish with butter and strain if desired.

name: Black Truffle Brioche French Toast with Foie Gras Custard, Compressed Berries & Maple-Sauternes Espuma
description: A decadent fine-dining brunch composition featuring brioche soaked in a foie gras-enriched custard, caramelized in beurre noisette, paired with vacuum-compressed berries, maple-Sauternes espuma, and finished with fresh black truffle and textural frozen fruit shards.
keywords: truffle french toast, foie gras custard, michelin brunch, compressed berries, maple espuma, molecular gastronomy, fine dining breakfast

steps.instruction: Prepare foie gras custard: Gently heat foie gras with cream until just melted. Blend until completely smooth. Whisk in egg yolks, sugar, salt, white pepper, and cognac. Pass through a fine chinois. Vacuum seal and cook in a precision immersion circulator at 63°C (145°F) for 45 minutes. Cool slightly.

Prepare compressed berries: Toss strawberries and raspberries with sugar, orange zest, and aged balsamic. Vacuum seal in a chamber vacuum sealer on high pressure to compress and intensify flavor. Chill.
Prepare maple-Sauternes espuma: Warm maple syrup and Sauternes together. Add cream and soy lecithin, then blend. Transfer to a Gourmet Whip, charge with 1 N2O cartridge, and hold warm.
Soak brioche: Submerge brioche slices in warm foie gras custard for 10–15 minutes until fully saturated but intact.
Cook French toast: Heat clarified butter in a heavy pan to 180°C (356°F). Sear brioche until deeply golden on both sides. Baste with brown butter to finish.
Prepare frozen berry shards: Blend a portion of compressed berries into a smooth purée. Spread thinly onto an anti-griddle to instantly freeze, then break into irregular shards.

Plating: Place French toast off-center. Arrange compressed berries around. Dispense maple-Sauternes espuma in controlled quenelles. Insert frozen berry shards vertically for height. Finish with freshly shaved black truffle, edible flowers, flaky sea salt, and optional gold leaf.

winePairings.name: Sauternes
winePairings.notes: Echoes the sweetness of the maple espuma while balancing foie gras richness with acidity.

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: Cuts through the fat and enhances the aromatic truffle and berry acidity.

name: Black Truffle Brown Butter Souffle Waffle
description: A Michelin-inspired waffle with a crisp caramelized exterior and airy souffle interior, layered with brown butter, black truffle, maple gastrique, and hazelnut texture.
keywords: waffle, truffle, brown butter, fine dining, gourmet, souffle waffle

steps.instruction: Prepare brown butter by heating unsalted butter until milk solids turn amber and aromatic. Strain and cool.
In a saucepan, reduce maple syrup, vinegar, star anise, and salt until a light syrup consistency forms. Remove star anise and set aside.
Toast hazelnuts, coat in caramelized sugar, cool, then grind into a fine dust.
Whisk flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt in a bowl.
In another bowl, whisk egg yolks with sugar until pale, then add milk, vanilla, truffle, and brown butter.
Combine wet and dry ingredients gently until just incorporated.
Whip egg whites to soft peaks and fold into batter in three stages to maintain airiness.
Cook batter in a preheated waffle iron until deep golden and crisp.
Plate waffle with whipped creme fraiche, drizzle maple gastrique, and finish with hazelnut dust, truffle shavings, and microgreens.

winePairings.name: Sauternes
winePairings.notes: Sweet botrytized wine balancing the truffle richness with acidity and honeyed depth.

winePairings.name: Champagne Brut Blanc de Blancs
winePairings.notes: High acidity and fine bubbles cut through the richness while enhancing nutty brown butter notes.

steps.instruction: Melt butter over medium heat until milk solids turn golden brown and aromatic, then strain.
Simmer all ingredients until slightly thickened. Remove star anise before serving.
Caramelize sugar, coat hazelnuts, cool completely, then grind into a fine powder.

name: Black Truffle Old Fashioned
description: A decadent Old Fashioned infused with black truffle and raw honey.
keywords: old fashioned, truffle, bourbon, honey, spirit-forward

steps.instruction: Stir all ingredients over ice for 15–20 seconds.
Strain over a large ice cube.
Express orange peel and serve.
Whisk hot water into honey until dissolved.
Stir in microplaned truffle and infuse 10 minutes.
Strain and refrigerate.

name: Brioche French Toast, Vanilla Espuma and Berry Coulis
description: A luxurious Michelin-inspired brunch dish featuring thick slices of artisanal brioche soaked in a fragrant vanilla bean and orange zest crème anglaise, pan-fried in clarified butter, finished with a crackling caramelized crust, and crowned with an ethereal vanilla espuma. Accompanied by macerated seasonal berries, toasted hazelnuts, and a vibrant berry coulis for perfect texture and flavor contrast.
keywords: french toast, brioche, pain perdu, espuma, fine dining, brunch

winePairings.name: Champagne Brut
winePairings.notes: Crisp bubbles cut through the richness and highlight the vanilla and berry notes.

winePairings.name: Late Harvest Riesling
winePairings.notes: Sweet floral notes complement the caramelized crust and macerated berries.

ingredients.item: Berry coulis or reduced maple syrup

specialtools.item: Gourmet Whip siphon (0.5 L) + 2 N?O chargers

steps.instruction: Prepare the infused custard base using a sous-vide immersion circulator at 60°C (140°F). Combine milk, cream, scraped vanilla bean and seeds, and microplaned orange zest. Infuse for 30 minutes.
In a bowl, gently whisk the eggs, caster sugar, and sea salt until combined. Strain the infused dairy through a chinois into the egg mixture. Add Grand Marnier if using. Refrigerate until needed.

Prepare the vanilla espuma: Whisk 100 ml heavy cream with vanilla bean paste and 20 g sugar until dissolved. Charge a siphon with two N?O cartridges, shake vigorously, and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes.
Macerate the berries: Toss 150 g mixed berries with 1 tbsp sugar and a squeeze of lemon juice. Let sit for 15 minutes.
Soak the brioche slices in the custard for 2–3 minutes per side until fully saturated but still holding shape. For deeper infusion, use a vacuum sealer on gentle setting.
Heat clarified butter in a heavy skillet over medium-low heat. Fry each soaked brioche slice for 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden and the interior reaches 70°C (158°F).

Remove to a wire rack. Immediately sprinkle each slice evenly with caster or turbinado sugar and caramelize the surface using a kitchen blowtorch in sweeping motions until a glassy, crackling crust forms.

Plating: Warm the plates. Place one or two slices of French toast slightly off-center. Dispense a generous cloud of vanilla espuma on top. Scatter macerated berries and crushed toasted hazelnuts around the base. Delicately apply flakes of edible gold leaf with tweezers. Garnish with micro edible flowers and finish with an artistic drizzle of berry coulis or reduced maple syrup.

name: Brown Butter Sablé with Tonka Bean Ganache
description: Crisp brown butter sablé cookies paired with a luxurious, aromatic tonka bean ganache for a balance of nutty, buttery crunch and creamy spiced chocolate.
keywords: brown butter, sablé, tonka bean, ganache, chocolate, pastry, dessert, fine dining

winePairings.name: Sauternes (half bottle)
winePairings.notes: Honeyed notes align with brown butter and vanilla.

winePairings.name: Tokaji Aszú 6 Puttonyos
winePairings.notes: Highly concentrated apricot sweetness matches richness.

steps.instruction: Pipe tonka bean ganache onto half of the sablés.
Top with a second sablé to form sandwich cookies or serve open-faced.
Garnish with cocoa nibs and a touch of gold leaf for an elegant finish.
Serve slightly chilled or at room temperature for the ideal texture contrast.
Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat until it turns golden brown and releases a nutty aroma. Cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine flour, almond flour, powdered sugar, and salt.
Add brown butter, egg yolk, and vanilla; mix until dough just comes together.
Shape into a log, wrap in plastic, and chill for 1 hour.
Slice into ½-inch rounds and bake at 170°C (340°F) for 12–14 minutes until golden brown at edges.
Heat cream with grated tonka bean just to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain.  Allow to cool for full flavor infusion.
Reheat cream and pour over chopped chocolate. Let sit for 1 minute.
Stir gently from the center until smooth and emulsified.
Incorporate butter and a pinch of salt until glossy.
Chill until thickened enough to pipe or spread.

name: Buckwheat Brioche with Cultured Butter
description: A nutty, deeply aromatic buckwheat brioche paired with tangy house-cultured butter.
keywords: brioche, buckwheat, cultured butter, fermentation, fine dining

winePairings.name: Muscadet
winePairings.notes: Crisp minerality cuts through butter richness and complements nutty buckwheat.

winePairings.name: Krug Grande Cuvée
winePairings.notes: Toasty brioche notes mirror the bread perfectly.

steps.instruction: Slice warm brioche and serve with quenelle of cultured butter.
Combine flours, sugar, salt, yeast in mixer.
Add eggs and milk; mix until dough forms.
Incorporate butter slowly; mix 8–10 minutes until glossy.
Shape and place in brioche mold; final proof 1 hour.
Proof at 37°C (100°F) 1.5 hours until doubled.
steps.note: If your oven does not have a proof option, use a proofing box

steps.instruction: Bake at 180°C (355°F) for 25–30 minutes.
Mix cream and buttermilk; ferment 24 hours at room temperature.
Chill, then whip until fat separates.
Strain through cheesecloth; knead in salt.

name: Burrata Sphere with Basil Oil & Tomato Water Snow
description: A delicate burrata sphere paired with aromatic basil oil and refreshing tomato water snow, combining creamy, herbal, and acidic elements.
keywords: burrata, basil oil, tomato water, molecular gastronomy, modernist, cold dish

winePairings.name: Rosé from Provence
winePairings.notes: Crisp strawberry + herb notes match basil/tomato.

winePairings.name: Sancerre Les Monts Damnés
winePairings.notes: High-end Sauvignon Blanc = precision + minerality.

steps.instruction: Spoon a small pool of basil oil on the plate. Place the burrata sphere in the center.
Grate tomato water snow over and around the sphere right before serving.
Garnish with micro basil leaves, cracked black pepper, or a drizzle of olive oil. Serve immediately.

ingredients.note: 1 g sodium alginate per 500 mL water
5 g calcium lactate per 1 L water

steps.instruction: Open the burrata and remove the stracciatella filling.
Blend the stracciatella, cream, salt, and sodium alginate until completely smooth.
Chill 30 minutes to remove bubbles.
Using a round spoon or mold, form small portions and drop into the calcium lactate bath for about 2 minutes.
Rinse gently in cold water to remove any excess calcium.
Set aside in a little olive oil to prevent sticking.
Alternative: Skip steps 1-6 and use the burrata as is for a simpler presentation with similar flavor.

ingredients.item: Neutral oil (grapeseed or light olive oil)

steps.instruction: Blanch basil leaves in boiling water for 10 seconds, then shock in ice water.
Squeeze dry, then blend with oil and salt until bright green.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Store refrigerated up to 5 days.
Blend tomatoes with salt and sugar.
Line a bowl with cheesecloth, pour the purée in, and let it drip overnight in the fridge to collect tomato water.
Mix tomato water with Xanthan gum and freeze tomato water solid.

name: Butter-Poached Blue Prawns with Saffron-Shellfish Emulsion
description: A molecular gastronomy course featuring precision-temperature prawns and a centrifuged saffron-shellfish foam.
keywords: prawns, shrimp, molecular gastronomy, sous vide

steps.instruction: Prepare the shellfish stock using prawn heads and shells.
Pour the strained stock into the canisters of a culinary centrifuge; spin until the liquid clarifies into a pure essence.
Place butter, saffron, and lemongrass into a vacuum bag; use a chamber vacuum sealer to pull a full vacuum for precision infusion.
Set the immersion circulator to 125.6�F (52�C).
Clean prawns and place in a vacuum bag with the saffron-infused butter; seal and submerge in the water bath for 12�15 minutes.
Reduce the centrifuged shellfish essence with white wine, using a digital refractometer to verify the ideal density/sugar concentration.
Add lecithin or heavy cream to the reduction; pour into a siphon and charge with gas cartridges.
Remove prawns from the bath, pat dry, and give a brief singe with a culinary torch for aroma.
Plating: Place prawns in a shallow bowl, dispense saffron foam from the siphon over the top, and garnish with chervil.

name: Butter-Poached Lobster with Saffron Foam, Fennel Ash & Citrus Gel
description: A refined seafood dish featuring butter-poached lobster, airy saffron foam, bright citrus gel, and fennel ash for a modern, Michelin-style presentation.
keywords: lobster, seafood, modernist, fine dining

steps.instruction: Remove lobster meat from shells carefully, keeping pieces intact.
Place lobster in a vacuum bag with butter, garlic, thyme, and lemon zest, then seal.
Cook in a water bath at 56�C (132.8�F) for 20�25 minutes.
Gently heat seafood stock, cream, and saffron to infuse flavor without boiling.
Strain the mixture, add lecithin, blend, and transfer to a cream siphon. Charge and keep warm.
Combine orange juice, lemon juice, and agar-agar, then bring to a boil while whisking.
Pour into a shallow container and allow to set.
Blend the set gel until smooth and strain for a refined texture.
Lightly coat fennel fronds in oil and dehydrate until fully dry.
Char the dried fennel and grind into a fine ash.
Remove lobster from bag and slice into even medallions.
Plate lobster with space between pieces for a clean presentation.
Add dots or lines of citrus gel around the plate.
Dispense saffron foam lightly over or beside the lobster.
Dust fennel ash sparingly across the plate.
Finish with microgreens, shaved fennel, and citrus segments for garnish.

winePairings.name: Chablis
winePairings.notes: Crisp minerality enhances the sweetness of lobster.

winePairings.name: Champagne
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and bubbles cut through the richness of butter.

name: Cacao Sablé with Fleur de Sel Caramel
description: A refined Parisian dessert combining a crisp cacao sablé base with a luscious fleur de sel caramel filling, a balance between bitterness, sweetness, and salt.
keywords: cacao, sablé, caramel, fleur de sel, french, pastry, Alain Ducasse

steps.instruction: Place cooled cacao sablé rounds on a wire rack or plate.
Spoon or pipe a layer of warm caramel over each sablé.
Allow caramel to set slightly at room temperature.
Finish with a light sprinkle of fleur de sel and optional chocolate shavings or gold leaf.
Serve at room temperature to enjoy the textural contrast between crisp sablé and soft caramel.
Cream butter and powdered sugar until smooth and pale.
Add egg yolk and mix to combine.
Sift flour, cocoa powder, and salt together; add to butter mixture.
Mix until dough comes together, then shape into a disk and chill 30 minutes.
Roll out to 4 mm thickness and cut into rounds using ring molds.
Bake at 170°C (340°F) for 10–12 minutes until firm. Cool completely.
In a saucepan, heat sugar and glucose until it melts and turns deep amber.
Carefully whisk in warm cream in stages — mixture will bubble vigorously.
Remove from heat and whisk in butter until smooth and glossy.
Add fleur de sel and stir to combine.
Cool slightly until thickened but still pourable.

name: Cacio e Pepe Ravioli with Truffle Essence
description: Refined ravioli filled with Pecorino Romano and mascarpone, finished in a truffle essence sauce — a luxurious interpretation of Rome’s classic cacio e pepe.
keywords: ravioli, pasta, truffle, pecorino, fine dining, italian

steps.instruction: Cut pasta rounds, fill with cheese mix, and seal to form ravioli.

Place a round on top and seal edges, making sure no air is trapped.
Boil ravioli for 2–3 minutes until tender.
Toss gently in truffle essence sauce.
Arrange on plate, spoon over sauce, and garnish with shaved truffle and pepper.
Mix flour and salt, make a well, and add eggs and oil.
Knead until smooth and elastic, about 8–10 minutes.
Wrap and rest 30 minutes before rolling thin sheets about 2 mm thick.
Combine all ingredients in a bowl and mix until smooth.
Adjust texture with a small amount of cream if needed.
Reduce stock and cream together to a glaze.
Whisk in truffle paste and finish with butter.
Season lightly and keep warm.

name: Caramelized Banana Mille-Feuille with Espresso Custard, Tonka Bean Air & Gold Leaf Honeycomb
description: A Michelin-level brunch dessert featuring vacuum-compressed caramelized banana layers stacked with espresso custard sheets, paired with tonka bean air, gold-leaf honeycomb shards, and aromatic banana essence.
keywords: banana mille-feuille, espresso custard, tonka bean foam, molecular gastronomy, fine dining brunch, honeycomb candy, modernist dessert

steps.instruction: Prepare vacuum-compressed bananas: Slice bananas into thin planks and seal in a vacuum chamber at full pressure to intensify flavor and density. Rest 10 minutes.
Caramelize banana layers: Heat sugar and glucose to 170°C (338°F). Add butter and salt, then coat banana slices. Lay flat and lightly torch for a glossy finish.
Prepare espresso custard: Heat cream, milk, and espresso. Temper into egg yolks and sugar. Cook to 82°C (180°F), whisk in agar, and set in thin sheets. Chill and cut into precise layers.
Create honeycomb: Cook sugar, honey, and water to 150°C (302°F). Remove from heat, whisk in baking soda, and pour onto a silicone mat. Cool, break into shards, and apply gold leaf.
Prepare tonka bean air: Infuse warm milk with tonka bean, strain, and add lecithin. Use an ultrasonic homogenizer to generate a stable, fine foam.
Optional banana crisp: Flash freeze thin banana slices in liquid nitrogen and shatter into delicate shards.

Plating: Stack alternating banana and custard layers into a mille-feuille. Arrange honeycomb shards vertically. Spoon tonka air alongside. Garnish with cocoa nibs, espresso reduction, and micro mint. Finish with aromatic banana mist if available.

winePairings.name: Espresso Martini
winePairings.notes: Enhances the coffee bitterness and complements the dessert’s layered richness.

name: Caramelized Vanilla Bean & Brown Butter Cinnamon Rolls
description: A Michelin-inspired laminated brioche cinnamon roll with caramelized brown butter filling, Ceylon cinnamon, citrus aromatics, and a mascarpone vanilla bean glaze.

steps.instruction: Combine flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a stand mixer.

Gradually incorporate butter and knead until smooth and elastic (windowpane stage).
Proof dough at 75�78�F until doubled, about 90 minutes.
Refrigerate dough overnight for flavor development and easier handling.
Prepare brown butter by heating butter until amber and nutty; cool slightly and mix with brown sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla, and salt.
Roll dough into a rectangle, spread filling evenly, and fold into thirds.

name: Caviar Cloud Eggs with Truffle Brioche & Brown Butter Hollandaise Foam
description: A Michelin-level brunch reimagining eggs Benedict: airy whipped egg white clouds encasing a molten yolk, set atop vacuum-infused truffle brioche, finished with brown butter hollandaise foam, herb emulsion accents, and premium caviar.
keywords: cloud eggs, truffle brioche, caviar brunch, michelin style breakfast, hollandaise foam, molecular gastronomy, fine dining eggs

steps.instruction: Prepare sous vide yolks: Place egg yolks with a small amount of clarified butter into a vacuum bag. Cook in a precision immersion circulator at 63°C (145°F) for 35 minutes until custard-like.

Make herb emulsion: Blend herbs, neutral oil, spinach, and lemon zest until smooth. Strain through a fine chinois. Spread onto an anti-griddle to create delicate frozen sheets, then break into organic shapes.
Infuse brioche: Lightly butter brioche slices and layer with shaved black truffle. Vacuum seal to compress and infuse flavor. Toast gently until golden and aromatic.

Prepare brown butter hollandaise: Brown butter until nutty. Whisk egg yolks with champagne vinegar over bain-marie until thickened. Slowly emulsify in brown butter, then add lemon juice and cayenne. Strain and transfer to a siphon, charge with N2O, and keep warm.
Create cloud eggs: Whip egg whites with cream of tartar to stiff peaks. Spoon into nests on parchment and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 3 minutes. Add yolk center and bake an additional 2 minutes.
Assembly: Place toasted truffle brioche on plate, top with cloud egg, and carefully insert sous vide yolk.
Plating: Dispense hollandaise foam tableside, add a quenelle of caviar, arrange herb emulsion pieces, microgreens, and edible flowers. Finish with gold leaf if desired.

winePairings.notes: High acidity cuts through the richness of hollandaise and enhances the caviar.

name: Chocolate Chip Cookie Tuile with Ice Cream Sandwich

name: Compressed Melon, Prosciutto Air, and Basil Pearls

name: Corn Crème Brûlée

keywords: corn, side, creamy, crème brûlée, fine dining

ingredients.item: Ears of Yellow or Bi-color Corn

specialtools.item: Crème Brûlée Pan and Ramekin set

steps.instruction: Set oven rack in the center of oven. Remove any other racks from oven. Place the crème brûlée pan (or large casserole dish) on rack and fill 1 inch deep with warm water. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
Cut corn kernels from cobs using a sharp knife. Divide corn kernels in half.
Using the back of the knife, scrape cobs to extract any remaining pulp. Discard cobs.
Place pulp, half of the corn kernels, half & half, 2 tablespoons of sugar, salt, and white pepper in a metal bowl. Blend until corn is completely dissolved.
Strain corn mixture through a fine mesh strainer into a mixing bowl, using the back of a spoon to press out any remaining solids.
Mix heavy cream into strained corn mixture. Taste for seasoning and adjust with more sugar, salt, and/or white pepper.
steps.note: Mixture should be sweet, but not excessive.

steps.instruction: Whisk egg yolks into corn mixture to fully incorporate.
steps.note: Be careful not to make lots of bubbles.

steps.instruction: Divide other half of corn kernels equally into four 4-ounce ramekins, and fill ramekins almost to top with corn mixture.
Carefully place ramekins into water bath in oven. Bake until custards are just set, start checking about 25-30 minutes into the bake.
Carefully remove the entire water bath from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
Remove from the water bath and refrigerate for about 4 hours.
Before serving, place ramekins back in the water bath in the 325 degree oven. Let warm for 10 minutes.
Remove from oven and lightly sprinkle with 2 tablespoons of sugar. Quickly pass over sugar with a blowtorch or burn with a brûlée iron. Serve immediately.

name: Dirty Vege-Burger with Smoky Mushroom Demi-Glace and Fermented Aioli

winePairings.name: Chardonnay (unoaked or lightly oaked)

drained and roughly mashed, reserve 2 tbsp liquid

ingredients.item: Olive oil, sea salt, black pepper

ingredients.item: Ripe avocado, heirloom tomato, microgreens, edible flower

steps.instruction: Weigh all ingredients precisely using the digital kitchen scale.

steps.instruction: Toss 300g mushrooms, onion, 2 garlic cloves, and carrot with olive oil, salt, pepper, and spices. Roast at 200°C (400°F) for 20–25 minutes until deeply caramelized. Reserve roasting juices.

Divide mixture into four equal portions (~180–200g each) by weight. Use the burger patty press to form uniform 2cm-thick patties.

Heat a cast-iron pan very hot with oil. Use the fish spatula to place and flip patties. Sear 4–5 minutes per side to develop a deep crust. Check internal temperature with the precision digital thermometer (target 70–75°C / 160–165°F).
Use the mandoline slicer (1–2mm setting) for paper-thin red onions (quick-pickle in rice vinegar, sugar, salt) and shallots (fry until golden and crispy).

name: Dry-Aged Hamachi Crudo with Yuzu-Kosho Snow, Compressed Cucumber & Smoked Olive Oil Pearls
description: A Michelin-level seafood starter featuring delicate slices of dry-aged hamachi, bright yuzu-kosho snow, vacuum-compressed cucumber, and smoked olive oil pearls. A refined balance of acidity, fat, texture, and aroma using modernist techniques.
keywords: hamachi crudo, yuzu kosho, molecular gastronomy, fine dining seafood, spherification, compressed cucumber, modernist cuisine

steps.instruction: Lightly cure the hamachi: Brush with white soy sauce and a few drops of yuzu juice. Rest for 10 minutes, then chill. Slice into thin, precise pieces using a sharp yanagiba knife.
Prepare yuzu-kosho base: Gently heat yuzu juice, sugar, and yuzu kosho until dissolved. Add gelatin (or agar) and set into a thin sheet. Chill until firm.
Create yuzu-kosho snow: Place the set gel onto an anti-griddle or freeze completely, then scrape into fine crystalline flakes.
Compress cucumber: Slice cucumber into uniform shapes. Vacuum seal with rice vinegar, simple syrup, and dill. Allow to compress until translucent, then chill.
Smoke olive oil: Place olive oil under a cloche and infuse with applewood smoke using a smoking gun for 2–3 minutes.
Prepare spherification bath: Mix calcium chloride with distilled water. Separately blend smoked olive oil with sodium alginate until smooth.
Form olive oil pearls: Using a pipette, drop olive oil mixture into calcium bath. Allow spheres to form, then rinse in clean water.

Plating: Arrange hamachi slices in a clean line or spiral. Add compressed cucumber pieces with geometric precision. Dot smoked olive oil pearls across the plate. Garnish with finger lime pearls, black sesame, micro shiso, and freshly grated wasabi.
Finish: Lightly scatter yuzu-kosho snow over the hamachi just before serving. Optionally cover with a cloche and infuse with a final touch of smoke tableside.

winePairings.name: Sancerre (Loire Valley Sauvignon Blanc)

name: Duck Carnitas with Mole Negro and Cocoa Nib Dust

steps.instruction: Spoon a layer of warm mole negro onto each plate.

Add duck legs and cook gently over low heat or in oven at 140°C (285°F) for 2–3 hours until and internal temperature of 63°C (145°F).
Remove duck and shred meat into bite-sized pieces.
Crisp shredded duck in a hot skillet for 2–3 minutes before plating.
Toast chiles lightly, then soak in hot water until softened.

name: Roasted Brussels Sprouts Pavé with Aged Balsamic Emulsion and Crispy Leaves
description: A structural culinary masterpiece featuring precisely layered and compressed Brussels sprouts, slow-roasted to achieve velvet-tender hearts with glass-like crispy exteriors. Finished with a glossy aged balsamic reduction, lemon zest, and accompanied by a light truffle-scented foam and crispy sprout leaf chips.
keywords: brussels sprouts, michelin style, roasted pavé, aged balsamic, molecular gastronomy, haute cuisine

ingredients.note: thinly sliced on a Japanese mandoline

steps.instruction: Trim Brussels sprouts and separate outer leaves for crispy chips. Halve the remaining sprouts lengthwise. Blanch halves in heavily salted boiling water for 2-3 minutes, shock in ice bath, and dry thoroughly using a high-capacity salad spinner.
Using a Japanese mandoline, slice shallots paper-thin. Toss sprout halves and reserved leaves separately with clarified butter, avocado oil, crushed garlic, thyme, sea salt, and pepper.

Preheat a heavy carbon steel or All-Clad baking sheet in a 230°C (450°F) oven. Arrange sprout halves cut-side down in a single layer. Vacuum-seal the layered halves in a chamber vacuum sealer for compression if desired, or arrange tightly in a terrine mold.

Roast at 230°C for 20-25 minutes until deeply caramelized with mahogany edges. Remove crispy leaves earlier when golden and set aside. In the final 5 minutes, brush lightly with aged balsamic for extra glaze.

Prepare the emulsion: Reduce aged balsamic with lemon juice and a touch of honey. For an aerated touch, warm heavy cream with truffle oil, blend, and charge in a Gourmet Whip with one N2O canister. Hold at 60°C.
Portioning: Cut the roasted pavé into precise rectangular portions or serve whole halves. Sear cut sides again briefly in a hot carbon steel pan for extra crispness.

Plating: Place the Brussels pavé centerpiece on a pristine white plate. Dispense a delicate cloud of truffle emulsion beside it. Artfully arrange crispy sprout leaves, toasted nuts, grated Parmigiano, lemon zest, and Maldon flakes. Finish with micro-thyme and a final balsamic drizzle.

steps.note: see sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Lightly season patties.
Vacuum seal using Vacuum Sealer Machine
Cook in water bath with Sous Vide Precision Cooker: 131°F (55°C) for 45 minutes.
Cook onions in butter over low heat for 25–30 minutes.
Add sugar halfway; finish with balsamic.
Blend until smooth.
Strain through fine mesh strainer.
Mix mayonnaise, truffle oil,  Dijon mustard, garlic and lemon juice
Strain for smoothness.
Transfer to squeeze bottle
Remove patties from sous vide, pat completely dry.
Sear in very hot pan (30–45 sec per side).
Add cheese during final seconds
Use hand torch to melt the cheese evenly and add controlled char
Quickly sear foie gras (30 sec per side)
Sauté mushrooms in butter + thyme until golden
Butter cut sides
Toast until golden and crisp
Place ring mold on plate and build
Bottom bun + thin truffle aioli layer.
Pipe onion purée neatly.
Add lettuce
Place burger patty with melted cheese
Add foie gras and/or mushrooms
Tomato slice
Microgreens
Top bun
Carefully remove ring mold for a clean, vertical stack
Place burger slightly off-center on a stone or matte plate
Pipe aioli dots using squeeze bottle
Add balsamic reduction drops around plate
Serve fries in a small metal cup
Garnish with microgreens, microplaned cheese (using Microplane), and Flaky Salt

name: Toasted Coconut & Rum Nightcap
description: A warm, silky dessert cocktail with toasted coconut, dark rum, and vanilla.
keywords: coconut, rum, dessert, creamy

steps.instruction: Shake all ingredients with ice until creamy.
Strain into a chilled rocks glass.
Simmer all ingredients for 15 minutes.
Blend until smooth.
Strain and cool.

name: Truffle Milk Bread Rolls
source: adapted from The French Laundry (Yountville, USA)
description: Ultra-soft Japanese milk bread rolls infused with black truffle aroma.
keywords: milk bread, truffle, shokupan, brioche

winePairings.name: Franciacorta Brut
winePairings.notes: Light bubbles and acidity lift truffle aromatics.

winePairings.name: Bourgogne Blanc from Leflaive
winePairings.notes: Silky, luxurious complement to truffle richness.

steps.instruction: Mix flour, milk, butter, sugar, yeast, salt, and truffle paste; knead until elastic.
Proof 1 hour; shape rolls.
Final proof 45 minutes, bake 180°C (350°F).
Brush rolls with melted butter and shave truffle over top.

name: Truffled Sous Vide Eggs Benedict with Smoked Salmon, Hollandaise Espuma & Ossetra Caviar
description: An ultra-luxurious brunch dish featuring precision sous-vide eggs, a weightless truffle hollandaise espuma, elegant smoked salmon rosettes, and a crowning of Ossetra caviar. Contrasting temperatures and textures create a three-star experience at home.
keywords: eggs benedict, sous vide, truffle, caviar, hollandaise espuma, smoked salmon, michelin

winePairings.name: Champagne (e.g., Krug Grande Cuvée)
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and fine bubbles cut through the richness while complementing the truffle and caviar.

winePairings.name: Chablis or White Burgundy
winePairings.notes: Crisp minerality balances the buttery hollandaise and salmon.

ingredients.item: White truffle oil (or grated fresh black truffle)
Fine sea salt and white pepper

steps.instruction: Set up sous-vide bath to 63.5 °C (146.3 °F). Gently lower the 4 eggs (in shells) into the bath and cook for 45–60 minutes.
steps.note: This yields a silky white and molten custardy yolk.

steps.instruction: Prepare truffle hollandaise base: Whisk 3 egg yolks, lemon juice, salt, and white pepper over a double boiler until pale and ribbon-like. Slowly whisk in warm clarified butter until thick. Stir in truffle oil or grated fresh truffle. Cool to room temperature.
Whip heavy cream to soft peaks. Gently fold in cooled hollandaise. Pour into siphon, charge with two N?O cartridges, shake vigorously, and refrigerate upright for 30 minutes.
Toast brioche until golden. Blanch asparagus in boiling salted water for 90 seconds, shock in ice water, and pat dry.
Warm plates. Place a ring mold on each plate, add toasted brioche, then layer smoked salmon in an elegant rosette.
Carefully crack and slide one sous-vide egg onto the salmon per plate using an offset spatula. Remove ring mold.
Dispense a generous cloud of truffle hollandaise espuma over each egg. Top with a quenelle of Ossetra caviar.

Shave 3–4 paper-thin slices of fresh black truffle directly over the dish. Arrange asparagus spears artistically to the side. Scatter microgreens and edible gold leaf. Add sauce dots for visual rhythm.

name: Vanilla Bean Espresso Cloud
description: A refined espresso martini with vanilla bean syrup and a light foam cap.
keywords: espresso, vanilla bean, dessert, martini

steps.instruction: Shake vodka, liqueur, espresso, and vanilla syrup vigorously with ice.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Top gently with Espresso Foam.
Simmer sugar and water until dissolved.
Add vanilla bean and infuse 20 minutes.
Strain and cool.
Froth hot espresso with sugar until foam forms.
Spoon gently onto the drink.

name: Vegetable Consommé with Edible Flower Ravioli
description: A crystal-clear vegetable consommé served with delicate ravioli filled with ricotta and edible flowers, creating a visually stunning and aromatic dish.
keywords: vegetarian, consommé, ravioli, edible flowers, fine dining, delicate, clear soup

winePairings.name: Grüner Veltliner
winePairings.notes: White pepper and acidity match delicate broth.

winePairings.name: Chablis Premier Cru
winePairings.notes: Pure, mineral, elegant with floral notes.

steps.instruction: Place ravioli in the center of a shallow bowl.
Gently pour hot vegetable consommé around the ravioli.
Garnish with flower petals and a drizzle of chive oil.
Serve immediately for a delicate and aromatic presentation.
Combine vegetables, tomato paste, and cold water in a large pot. Simmer for 45–60 minutes to extract flavor.
Strain through fine mesh sieve.
Whisk egg whites and add to strained broth. Slowly bring to a simmer to form a raft that clarifies the liquid.
Carefully ladle the clear consommé from under the raft.
Season lightly with salt before serving.

ingredients.note: nasturtiums, pansies, or violets, finely chopped

steps.instruction: Make pasta dough by mixing flour and eggs; knead until smooth. Rest for 30 minutes.
Roll dough into thin sheets using pasta roller.
Combine ricotta, Parmesan, and chopped edible flowers for filling.
Spoon small portions of filling onto pasta sheet, cover with another sheet, and seal edges.
Cut into ravioli shapes and cook gently in salted boiling water for 2–3 minutes.

name: Volaille de Bresse en Vessie
description: Bresse chicken poached in a bladder, served with creamy sauce suprême and truffle.
keywords: bocuse, classic french, bresse chicken, truffle, bladder poaching

ingredients.note: If you cannot find a Bresse chicken, use a high quality whole chicken.
a sealable plastic bag may be used as an alternative

steps.instruction: Stuff the chicken cavity with truffle slices.
Seal chicken inside bladder with kitchen twine.  Folder over the top and tie again to make sure no air can escape.
Place the sealed bladder in a large stock pot with chicken broth.  Simmer for about 1.5 hours.
Ladle broth over the bladder periodically to keep it moist.
Serve carved chicken with sauce suprême and truffle slices.
Reduce stock; whisk in cream and butter.

name: Wagyu Truffle Hot Dog – Le Chien Chaud de Luxe
description: An ultra-luxury reinterpretation of the classic hot dog: A5 Wagyu beef sausage cooked sous-vide, nestled in a vacuum-smoked brioche bun, finished with foie-gras espuma, hand-shaved black Périgord truffle, Ossetra caviar, and smoked truffle aioli. A three-Michelin-star experience in one perfect bite.
keywords: hot dog, wagyu, truffle, foie gras, caviar, fine dining, luxury

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne (e.g., Dom Pérignon)
winePairings.notes: Bubbles cut through the rich foie gras and truffle while enhancing the Wagyu savoriness.

winePairings.name: Rare Japanese Whisky (e.g., Yamazaki 18)
winePairings.notes: Smoky depth complements the cold-smoked elements and umami explosion.

steps.instruction: Prepare the Foie Gras Espuma and Smoked Truffle Aioli
steps.note: see sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Prepare the Vacuum-Smoked Brioche Buns
steps.note: see sub-recipe

steps.instruction: Sous-vide the Wagyu sausages in the Polyscience circulator at exactly 60°C (140°F) for 45 minutes.
Shock sausages in ice water for 2 minutes, pat dry, then sear briefly with the Searzall torch or hot pan for Maillard crust.
Using precision tweezers and plating tools, begin assembly on a pristine white plate.
Spread smoked truffle aioli inside the warm smoked bun, nestle the hot sausage, and pipe a ribbon of foie-gras espuma from the siphon.
Shave 5–6 paper-thin slices of black truffle directly onto the espuma using the Brevetti slicer.
Add tiny quenelles of Ossetra caviar with tweezers, microgreens, and 2–3 flakes of edible gold leaf. Optional: artistic swipe of aged balsamic.
Serve immediately with negative space for dramatic Michelin-style presentation.
Sear foie gras with shallot until lightly caramelized, deglaze with Cognac.
Blend to silky smoothness in Vitamix, strain through chinois, season, and charge siphon with two N?O cartridges. Refrigerate upright for at least 2 hours.
Emulsify classic aioli base in high-speed blender.
Infuse with applewood smoke using the Smoking Gun (seal and shake twice for deep flavor).
Place each bun in a vacuum bag, fill with applewood smoke using the Smoking Gun Pro.
Immediately seal on chamber vacuum sealer and rest 20–30 minutes for smoke penetration.
Remove and lightly toast insides just before assembly.

name: White Tea & Honey Fizz
description: A light, floral aperitif built on white tea, gin, and bubbles.
keywords: white tea, honey, aperitif, gin, fizz

steps.instruction: Shake gin, syrup, and lemon juice with ice.
Strain into a chilled glass and top with sparkling water.
Garnish with an edible flower.
Steep the white tea in hot water for 4 minutes.
Strain and whisk in honey until dissolved.
Cool and refrigerate.

name: World-Class Chef-Inspired Avocado Toast
description: Elevated avocado toast featuring creamy textured avocado mash on thick sourdough, topped with precision-cooked 63°C egg, confit cherry tomatoes, toasted pine nuts, black sesame, and microgreens. A luxurious brunch dish inspired by fine-dining techniques.
keywords: avocado toast, sourdough, sous vide egg, gourmet breakfast, chef recipe
chef: Inspired by Gordon Ramsay & Bobby Flay techniques

ingredients.item: red pepper flakes or Aleppo pepper
toasted pine nuts or sliced almonds

ingredients.note: mixed with lemon zest and sea salt

steps.instruction: Prepare thick, even slices of sourdough using a precision bread knife. Toast until deeply golden and crisp on the outside, chewy inside. Optionally torch edges lightly for char.

Make the avocado base: In a mortar and pestle, gently crush avocado flesh with minced shallot, lime juice, lemon zest, chopped herbs, olive oil, chili flakes, salt, and pepper to a creamy yet chunky texture.
Cook eggs using sous-vide immersion circulator at 63–65°C for 45–60 minutes for silky custardy yolks. Keep warm.
Prepare confit cherry tomatoes by slow-cooking in olive oil with garlic and thyme at low temperature, or lightly torch fresh ones.

Assemble: Drizzle toasted bread with olive oil and flaky salt. Spread avocado mixture evenly. Top with egg, confit tomatoes, toasted nuts, black sesame blend, microgreens, and a final drizzle of olive oil or aged balsamic.
Serve immediately while toast is warm and crisp.

winePairings.name: Crisp Sauvignon Blanc
winePairings.notes: Bright acidity and citrus notes cut through the richness of the avocado and complement the lime and herbs perfectly.

winePairings.name: Chablis or unoaked Chardonnay
winePairings.notes: Mineral-driven white with subtle oak to match the toasted sourdough and nutty elements.

steps.instruction: Combine ingredients in a small saucepan and cook over very low heat (around 200°F/93°C) for 20-30 minutes until tomatoes are softened but hold shape.
Cool slightly and use as topping. Store leftover oil for other uses.

name: Yuzu-Glazed A5 Wagyu Tataki with Uni-Miso Purée, Gochujang Caramel, Thai Basil Espuma, and Crispy Rice Crumble
description: An exquisite Michelin-star Asian fusion course featuring premium A5 Japanese Wagyu lightly torched and glazed with yuzu, paired with silky uni-miso purée, spicy-sweet gochujang caramel, airy Thai basil espuma, and textural crispy rice crumble.
keywords: wagyu, tataki, asian fusion, uni, yuzu, michelin

winePairings.name: Junmai Daiginjo Sake
winePairings.notes: Clean, fruity notes cut through the rich wagyu and complement yuzu brightness.

winePairings.name: Alsace Riesling (dry or off-dry)
winePairings.notes: Crisp acidity balances the caramel heat and uni umami.

ingredients.item: A5 Japanese Wagyu ribeye or striploin
Fresh Hokkaido or Santa Barbara uni roe
Micro shiso leaves, edible gold leaf, yuzu zest, toasted black sesame seeds

steps.instruction: Prepare the Uni-Miso Purée, Gochujang Caramel, Thai Basil Espuma, and Crispy Rice Crumble
steps.note: see sub-recipes

steps.instruction: Season the A5 Wagyu lightly with sea salt. Vacuum-seal with yuzu juice, soy sauce, and yuzu kosho.
Cook in sous-vide water bath at exactly 52°C (125.6°F) for 60 minutes.
Shock in ice water for 10 minutes, pat completely dry, and reserve bag juices.
Brush Wagyu with reserved juices and torch the surface evenly until deep mahogany crust forms (10–15 seconds per side).
Slice against the grain into 5 mm thick pieces.
Plating Layer 1: Place three precise dots of Uni-Miso Purée on the plate.
Plating Layer 2: Arrange torched Wagyu slices in a gentle sweeping arc. Drizzle with warm Gochujang Caramel.
Plating Layer 3: Dispense two elegant mounds of Thai Basil Espuma. Scatter Crispy Rice Crumble sparingly.
Finish with micro shiso leaves, edible gold leaf flakes, microplaned yuzu zest, and toasted black sesame seeds.
Combine all ingredients in the mixing bowl.
Blend at speed 8 for 2 minutes, scraping sides once.
Pass through a fine chinois strainer for silky texture. Transfer to squeeze bottle and chill.
Combine all ingredients in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Stir until sugar dissolves and mixture reduces to glossy caramel (5–7 minutes).
Keep warm in bain-marie until plating.
Blend coconut milk, cream, basil, lecithin, and salt for 60 seconds.
Strain through fine sieve.
Pour into Gourmet Whip siphon, charge with two N?O cartridges, shaking vigorously. Refrigerate upright minimum 2 hours.
Spread cooled sushi rice thinly on silicone mat.
Heat oil to 180°C (356°F) and fry small clusters until golden and crisp (2–3 minutes).
Drain, cool slightly, and crush lightly into irregular crumbs. Hold warm until service.
